howItWorks.title: How This Capify Alternative Makes Lyric Videos

steps.title: Upload your song
steps.description: Drop an MP3, WAV, FLAC, AAC, OGG, or M4A file (up to 100MB). No account needed to start, and no manual lyric typing required. Try LyricVideoMaker with the same song you used in Capify and compare the results.

steps.title: AI transcribes and syncs
steps.description: AI transcribes lyrics from the audio automatically and syncs every line to the beat — in 50+ languages. Click any line to edit. AI also reads the song's mood to generate matching visuals.

steps.title: Preview and export
steps.description: Watch the full lyric video before publishing. Compare the preview quality, lyric accuracy, and visual feel against Capify. Export in 16:9 for YouTube, 9:16 for TikTok, Reels, and Shorts, or 1:1 for feed posts. Free to export, no subscription required.

whyChoose.title: Why Test Your Lyric Video on LyricVideoMaker
whyChoose.description: If you are comparing Capify with another lyric video maker, the best test is the same song. LyricVideoMaker focuses on the parts that decide whether the result works: lyric accuracy, beat timing, readable layout, and visuals that fit the track.

highlight.title: AI transcription + visuals, not just text on video
highlight.description: LyricVideoMaker transcribes lyrics automatically, syncs them to the beat, and generates visuals driven by the song's mood. You don't type lyrics or pick templates — upload a song and get a finished video.

cards.title: Compare from the same audio
cards.description: Use the exact track you already tested elsewhere, then judge the transcript, timing, and visual direction side by side.

cards.title: Mood visuals are part of the workflow
cards.description: LyricVideoMaker treats visuals as part of the song interpretation, not just a background layer behind text.

cards.title: Readable lyrics stay the benchmark
cards.description: The result is judged by whether fans can follow the words clearly across the moments that matter: verse, hook, chorus, and ending.

contentSections.heading: What is a Capify alternative?
contentSections.intro: A Capify alternative for lyric videos should be judged on the same song, not on a feature checklist alone. LyricVideoMaker is a song-first AI workflow: upload audio, get lyrics transcribed and synced, review the lines, generate mood-driven scenes, and export multiple MP4 formats.
bullets: Input is your audio file; no project import is required.
It can create editable lyrics, beat timing, AI scene art, preview, and MP4 export.
Output supports 16:9, 9:16, and 1:1 from the same lyric project.
The useful comparison is result quality: lyric accuracy, timing, readability, and style fit.

contentSections.heading: Capify alternative specs at a glance

rows: Accepted input
Audio upload only: MP3, WAV, FLAC, AAC, OGG, or M4A, up to 100MB per file.
Lyrics
AI transcribes vocals automatically in 50+ languages, then lets you edit every line before export.
Sync
Beat-aware line timing keeps lyrics readable and musical instead of simply dropping captions on a fixed grid.
Visuals
Storyboard planning and AI scene-image generation create backgrounds around the mood of the song.
Exports
MP4 with original audio in 16:9, 9:16, or 1:1 for YouTube, Shorts, TikTok, Reels, and feeds.
Start
Free credits are available at the start, so you can preview the workflow before spending more time on a release.

contentSections.heading: Capify vs LyricVideoMaker: what to compare
contentSections.intro: Because Capify is a closer AI lyric-video competitor, the fairest test is practical. Run the same song and compare the work left after the first render.

rows: First useful preview
How quickly can you see the whole song with readable lyrics?
Transcript cleanup
How many words, names, or repeated hooks need correction?
Beat sync
Do lines land with the vocal and musical phrasing?
Visual fit
Do generated scenes feel tied to the song rather than a generic mood?
Control after AI
Can you edit lines and timing without rebuilding the video?
Export coverage
Can one project produce widescreen, vertical, and square assets?

contentSections.heading: Best practices when comparing Capify alternatives
contentSections.intro: Use these checks before you render, especially when the same project needs more than one platform format.
bullets: Use the same audio file in both tools, preferably the final mix.
Ignore marketing claims until you compare lyric accuracy on your own song.
Check the first chorus for timing; hook drift is the easiest flaw to notice.
Look at readability on a phone, not only in a desktop preview.
Count the minutes spent fixing the project after AI generation.
Compare export variants, because release week rarely needs only one format.
Pick the workflow that leaves you with fewer manual timeline decisions.

contentSections.heading: When a direct AI alternative is worth testing
contentSections.intro: A Capify alternative lets you compare a song-first AI lyric workflow against another AI tool without starting from a blank timeline. The choices that matter happen before you render — here are the principles worth keeping.

cards.title: Compare workflows, not feature lists
cards.description: The real test of an alternative is whether audio-in transcription, beat-aware timing, and scene generation produce a video that sounds like your song — not which tool has the longer feature list.

cards.title: Lyrics are structure, not decoration
cards.description: The words are the structure the viewer follows, not a caption added last. Always transcribe first, align to the beat second, and design the scene third.

cards.title: AI does the heavy lifting, you keep the taste
cards.description: AI handles transcription, timing, and scene generation. Whether the style fits, a line break should change, or you need a vertical, square, or widescreen export stays your call.

cards.title: The scene serves the lyric
cards.description: Viewers should never choose between reading the line and watching the scene. Keep contrast strong, lines short, and motion restrained so the visuals support the words.

cards.title: Review in three passes
cards.description: Watch once for words to catch transcription errors, once for rhythm to catch lines that arrive early or late, and once for platform fit so it still reads in a phone feed.

cards.title: One source, every format
cards.description: A single project becomes a 16:9 full version, a 9:16 vertical, and a 1:1 square without retyping lyrics or rebuilding timing — which is what makes a release feel planned.

cards.title: Build it while the song is fresh
cards.description: Make the lyric video while the song is still in your ear: you will hear awkward line breaks faster, catch misheard words sooner, and judge where the energy should rise or stay quiet.

contentSections.heading: Capify alternative checklist before export
contentSections.intro: A quick pre-export review catches most quality problems while they are still cheap to fix.

rows: Audio readiness
Accepted formats are MP3, WAV, FLAC, AAC, OGG, and M4A; keep the file under 100MB.
Language review
The system auto-detects 50+ languages, but bilingual hooks and names deserve a manual pass.
Timing review
Beat sync gives a strong baseline; nudge any line that lands early or late against the vocal.
Scene review
Generated images should match the mood without making text hard to read.
Export review
Check 16:9, 9:16, and 1:1 separately because readable text size changes by format.

items.question: Is LyricVideoMaker free as a Capify alternative?
items.answer: Yes. LyricVideoMaker is free to use as a Capify alternative — free credits to start, export included, no subscription or credit card.

items.question: How is LyricVideoMaker different from Capify?
items.answer: LyricVideoMaker auto-transcribes lyrics from audio, syncs every line to the beat, generates mood-driven AI visuals, and exports in multiple formats — all from one upload.

items.question: Do I need to type lyrics like in Capify?
items.answer: No — unlike a type-it-yourself workflow, AI transcribes every lyric line from your audio in 50+ languages. You only review and edit.

items.question: Can I export for TikTok and YouTube?
items.answer: Yes. Export in 16:9 for YouTube, 9:16 for TikTok, Reels, and Shorts, or 1:1 for feed posts. All formats from one upload.

items.question: Can I use exported lyric videos commercially?
items.answer: Yes. Your exports are yours to monetize and release — the same freedom you'd expect from Capify. Just make sure you have the rights to the music you upload.

items.question: How hard is it to switch from Capify?
items.answer: There's nothing to migrate. Upload the same song you used in Capify and you'll have a finished lyric video in a couple of minutes — no project files to move, no setup.
